member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer
member.summary: Manages live broadcast relay for an . Handles OBS upload (PUT/POST) and RTMP publish ingestion, FLV muxing, viewer queue fan-out, byte recording, and serves an HTML player page. Call to wire up the default routes on the underlying server.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.ViewerQueueCapacity
member.summary: Maximum number of queued byte-array items per viewer before starts dropping frames. A larger value tolerates slow readers at the cost of memory.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.ViewerWriteBufferSize
member.summary: Size (in bytes) of the per-viewer write buffer used to batch small FLV tags into larger HTTP chunks. Defaults to 2 MB. Larger values produce fewer, bigger writes which improves throughput at the expense of latency.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.StreamKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the stream key required for RTMP publish connections. Publishers must supply this key to begin streaming. A random 24-character key is generated by default. Set to or empty to allow any key.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.GenerateStreamKey(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a cryptographically random stream key of the specified length using URL-safe alphanumeric characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9).

member.name: E:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.LogOutput
member.summary: Raised when the broadcast server wants to write a log message.

member.name: E:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.StatsUpdated
member.summary: Raised once per second (or whenever the consumer calls ) with the latest RTMP statistics snapshot.

member.summary: Creates a new attached to the given . Call to set up the default streaming routes.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.#ctor(SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer)
member.summary: Creates a new attached to a . HTTP streaming routes are registered via the server's handler. RTMP publish routes are registered on the base and handled via 's built-in RTMP protocol detection.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.BroadcastServer.Register
member.summary:
Registers the default streaming routes on the underlying :
GET /stream – HTML player page (mpegts.js)
GET /stream/data – raw FLV relay for the player / VLC
PUT /Upload and POST /Upload – OBS upload ingestion
RTMP wildcard publish route

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder

member.summary: Disables auto-generated directory listings (Options -Indexes).

member.summary: Enables auto-generated directory listings (Options +Indexes).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.DenyAll
member.summary: Denies all access to the directory (Require all denied).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.AllowAll
member.summary: Grants access to all clients (Require all granted).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.AllowFrom(System.String[])
member.summary:
Allows access from the specified IP addresses or CIDR ranges.
Example: .AllowFrom("192.168.1.0/24", "10.0.0.1")

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.DenyFrom(System.String[])
member.summary:
Denies access from the specified IP addresses or CIDR ranges.
Example: .DenyFrom("10.0.0.5")

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.Order(System.String)
member.summary: Sets the evaluation order for Allow/Deny rules. "deny,allow" = deny first, then allow overrides (default). "allow,deny" = allow first, then deny overrides.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.RequireBasicAuth(System.String,System.String[])
member.summary: Requires HTTP Basic authentication with the given realm and credentials. Credentials are stored as # AuthCredential user:password comment directives inside the .htaccess file.
member.param: The authentication realm displayed to the user.
One or more username/password pairs as "user:password" strings.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.DenyFiles(System.String[])
member.summary:
Denies access to files matching the given glob patterns.
Example: .DenyFiles("*.log", "*.bak", ".env")

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.AllowOnlyFiles(System.String[])
member.summary:
Restricts access to only the specified file patterns; all others are denied.
Example: .AllowOnlyFiles("*.html", "*.css", "*.js")

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder.AddHeader(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Adds a response header for all files served from this directory.
Example: .AddHeader("X-Frame-Options", "DENY")

member.summary: Appends a raw directive line to the .htaccess file.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpClient
member.summary: Minimal HTTP/HTTPS client supporting headers, content-length, chunked transfer, and redirects.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpClient.RegisterProtocol(SocketJack.Net.IProtocolHandler)
member.summary: Registers a protocol handler used for detecting response formats.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpClient.RemoveProtocol(SocketJack.Net.IProtocolHandler)
member.summary: Removes a previously registered protocol handler.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RouteHandler`1
member.summary: Typed route handler delegate. The request body is automatically deserialized to and passed as the parameter.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.StreamRouteHandler
member.summary: Delegate for streaming routes. The handler receives a that sends each as an HTTP chunk, keeping the connection open until the handler returns or calls .

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.UploadStreamRouteHandler
member.summary: Delegate for upload-streaming routes. The handler receives an from which it can read incoming data chunks (e.g., OBS video) as they arrive on the connection. The handler runs on a background task and should return when it is done consuming data or when returns .

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RtmpPublishHandler
member.summary: Delegate for RTMP publish routes. The handler receives an that yields media chunks as OBS (or another RTMP encoder) publishes. Each chunk is prefixed with a single byte indicating the RTMP message type (8 = audio, 9 = video, 18 = metadata) followed by the raw payload.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._activeStreamConnections
member.summary: Tracks connections that currently have an active handler running. Subsequent data on these connections is raw payload (e.g., OBS video) and must not be parsed as HTTP. The data still flows through for other subscribers to capture.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._activeUploadConnections
member.summary: Tracks connections that have an active handler running. Subsequent raw data on these connections is forwarded to the corresponding instead of being parsed as HTTP.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._requestBuffers
member.summary: Per-connection buffer for accumulating partial HTTP requests that span multiple TCP segments.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._activeRtmpConnections
member.summary: Tracks connections running the RTMP protocol (e.g., OBS publishing via rtmp://). Subsequent data on these connections is fed into the state machine instead of being parsed as HTTP.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.HttpTotalBytesSent
member.summary: Running total of bytes sent across all HTTP responses (including headers). This counter persists across connection lifetimes, unlike per-connection counters which are lost when short-lived HTTP connections close.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.HttpTotalBytesReceived
member.summary: Running total of bytes received across all HTTP requests.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._httpBytesSentSnapshot
member.summary: Bytes sent via HTTP responses since the last call to . Use this to compute an HTTP bytes-per-second rate from the WPF metrics timer.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer._httpBytesReceivedSnapshot
member.summary: Bytes received via HTTP requests since the last call to .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.SnapshotHttpBytesSent
member.summary: Returns the number of HTTP bytes sent since the last snapshot and resets the delta. Call once per second to get a bytes-per-second rate.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.SnapshotHttpBytesReceived
member.summary: Returns the number of HTTP bytes received since the last snapshot and resets the delta. Call once per second to get a bytes-per-second rate.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.Robots
member.summary: Gets or sets the content served at /robots.txt. Set to to disable the built-in robots.txt route. Defaults to a permissive policy.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.AllowDirectoryListing
member.summary: When , mapped directories that contain no index.html / index.htm will return an auto-generated HTML listing of their contents. Defaults to . A .htaccess file inside the directory can override this per-directory with Options +Indexes or Options -Indexes.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.CacheControl
member.summary: Gets or sets the Cache-Control header value added to every HTTP response. When (the default), no Cache-Control header is emitted. Example values: "public, max-age=3600", "no-cache", "private, max-age=86400". For static files served via , an ETag and Last-Modified header are also sent, and If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since conditional requests are honoured with 304 Not Modified.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.Map``1(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RouteHandler{``0})
member.summary: Maps a route whose handler receives a deserialized body of type . The request body is deserialized using the configured serializer before the handler is invoked.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapStream(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.StreamRouteHandler)
member.summary: Maps a streaming route. The handler receives a it can write to line-by-line. The connection stays open until the handler returns.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapUploadStream(System.String,System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.UploadStreamRouteHandler)
member.summary: Maps an upload-streaming route. When an incoming HTTP request matches, the handler runs on a background task and receives an that yields data chunks as they arrive (e.g., continuous OBS MPEG-TS video over HTTP POST).

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapRtmpPublish(System.String,SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RtmpPublishHandler)
member.summary: Maps an RTMP publish route. When an OBS (or compatible) encoder connects via RTMP and publishes to the given name, the runs on a background task and receives an with media data. Use "*" as a wildcard to match any app name.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapDirectory(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a local directory to a URL prefix so that files inside the directory are served when a GET (or HEAD) request matches. For example, MapDirectory("/static", @"C:\wwwroot") serves C:\wwwroot\style.css when the client requests /static/style.css. If is "/", the directory is served at the root.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local directory.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapDirectory(System.String,System.String,System.Action{SocketJack.Net.HtAccessBuilder})
member.summary: Maps a local directory to a URL prefix and configures a .htaccess file using the fluent API for directory-level security.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local directory.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RemoveDirectoryMapping(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously mapped directory route.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.MapFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Maps a URL path to a specific local file. When a GET (or HEAD) request matches the , the file at is served with the appropriate MIME type. For example, MapFile("/js/widget.js", @"C:\Pages\widget.js") serves the file when the client requests /js/widget.js.
member.param: The absolute or relative path to the local file.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.RemoveFileMapping(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a previously mapped file route.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.IsDirectoryListingAllowed(System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether directory listing is allowed for the given local directory. Checks for a .htaccess file first; if it contains Options +Indexes listing is allowed, Options -Indexes denies it. Falls back to .

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.GenerateDirectoryListing(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Generates an HTML page listing the contents of a directory.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.IsBodyComplete(SocketJack.Net.HttpRequest)
member.summary: Returns when the request body appears to be fully available in (as opposed to a streaming upload that arrives over multiple reads).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.ChunkedThreshold
member.summary: Minimum body size in bytes before the server switches to chunked transfer encoding. Bodies smaller than this are sent with Content-Length in a single write. Default is 8 KB.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.ChunkedSize
member.summary: Size of each chunk when using chunked transfer encoding. Default is 4 KB.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.CleanupHttpConnection(System.Guid)
member.summary: Cleans up per-connection HTTP state (buffers, active streams, uploads). Called on client disconnection.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.TrackHttpBytesSent(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection,System.Int32)
member.summary: Tracks bytes written directly to an HTTP connection's stream so that and the per-second counters remain accurate for HTTP traffic. Also updates the server-level aggregate counter.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpServer.ParseHttpRequest(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Parses a raw HTTP request string into an HttpRequest object.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpRequest.QueryString
member.summary: The raw query string from the request URL (everything after the ?), or if no query string was present.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpRequest.QueryParameters
member.summary: URL-decoded key/value pairs parsed from the query string. Both %XX and + (as space) encodings are handled.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.HttpResponse.StatusLine
member.summary: Builds the full HTTP status line (e.g. HTTP/1.1 200 OK), always falling back to HTTP/1.1 200 OK when any component is missing or empty.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.HttpResponse.WriteChunkedTo(System.IO.Stream,System.Int32,SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Writes the response to using HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer encoding. Each line of text is sent as its own chunk so the client can render progressively. For binary (non-text) bodies, falls back to fixed-size chunks.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.FileResponse
member.summary: Represents a binary file response from a route handler. Return an instance of this from a to send raw bytes (e.g., an image or PDF) with an explicit content type instead of the default JSON serialization behaviour.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.FileResponse.Data
member.summary: The raw response body bytes.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.FileResponse.ContentType
member.summary: The MIME content type (e.g., "image/png").

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.ChunkedStream
member.summary: Wraps a network stream with HTTP/1.1 chunked transfer encoding. Write lines or raw data; each write is sent as an individual chunk and flushed immediately so the client can render progressively. Call (or let the server call it) to send the terminating chunk.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.ChunkedStream.ContentType
member.summary: Gets or sets the Content-Type header for the chunked response. Must be set before the first call to , , or .

member.summary: Sends a single line of text (with trailing newline) as one HTTP chunk.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.ChunkedStream.Write(System.String)
member.summary: Sends text as one HTTP chunk without appending a newline.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.ChunkedStream.Write(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sends arbitrary bytes as one HTTP chunk.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.ChunkedStream.Finish
member.summary: Sends the terminating zero-length chunk. Called automatically when the stream route handler returns.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.UploadStream
member.summary: Provides a stream-like interface for reading incoming data chunks on an HTTP upload-streaming connection (e.g., OBS sending continuous MPEG-TS video via POST). Data is enqueued by the server as it arrives and can be consumed asynchronously by the .

member.summary: Marks the stream as complete. No more data will arrive.

member.summary: when the stream is complete and all queued data has been consumed.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.UploadStream.Connection
member.summary: The that this upload stream is associated with.

member.summary: Asynchronously reads the next chunk of data. Returns when the stream is complete or the connection is closed.

member.summary: Tries to read the next chunk of data synchronously. Returns when no data is available within the timeout or the stream is complete.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer
member.summary:
A TCP server that detects the incoming byte format (e.g. HTTP request vs SocketJack-framed JSON) and routes each connection to the appropriate . This allows a single listening port to serve multiple protocols simultaneously.
By default a and an are registered. Additional handlers can be added via ; they are evaluated in registration order, with built-in handlers checked last.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.#ctor(System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new that listens on the given port.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.#ctor(SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new with explicit options.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.RegisterProtocol(SocketJack.Net.IProtocolHandler)
member.summary: Registers a custom protocol handler. Custom handlers are evaluated before the built-in HTTP and SocketJack handlers.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.RemoveProtocol(SocketJack.Net.IProtocolHandler)
member.summary: Removes a previously registered custom protocol handler.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.Http
member.summary: Returns the built-in for configuring HTTP routes, directory mappings, and other HTTP-specific settings.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SocketJack
member.summary: Returns the built-in for reference. SocketJack-protocol connections are also handled through the normal event and registered callbacks.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.WebSocket
member.summary: Returns the built-in for configuring WebSocket-specific settings. WebSocket connections share the same serialization and callback pipeline as SocketJack connections but use RFC 6455 framing.

member.name: E:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SocketJackClientConnected
member.summary: Raised when a connection's first data is identified as SocketJack protocol. Use this instead of when you need to send SocketJack-framed objects only to SocketJack clients (not HTTP).

member.name: E:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.WebSocketClientConnected
member.summary: Raised when a WebSocket upgrade handshake completes successfully. Use this to perform initialization for browser-based WebSocket clients.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.SyncPeer(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Overrides peer synchronization so that only connections confirmed as SocketJack receive broadcast peer updates. Without this filter, pushes SocketJack-framed bytes into every connection's send queue — including HTTP connections — corrupting their response stream.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.Send(SocketJack.Net.P2P.Identifier,System.Object)
member.summary: Routes identifier-based sends to the correct protocol handler. On a standard , this wraps the object in a and sends to the server connection. Here we find the actual client connection and use the appropriate framing.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.InvokeWebSocketClientConnected(SocketJack.Net.NetworkConnection)
member.summary: Invoked by after a successful WebSocket handshake to raise the event.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.MutableTcpServer.RtmpDelegateHandler
member.summary: Lightweight handler that delegates RTMP connections to the base method, which contains the full RTMP handshake, session, and publish-route pipeline.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.SocketJackProtocolHandler
member.summary: Detects and processes SocketJack length-prefixed framed messages. When receives raw bytes that begin with a 15-byte padded length header, this handler extracts the payload, deserializes it through the SocketJack serializer, and dispatches it through the normal pipeline.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.SocketJackProtocolHandler._buffers
member.summary: Per-connection buffer for accumulating partial SocketJack frames.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.SocketJackProtocolHandler.CanHandle(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Returns if starts with a 15-byte NUL-padded numeric length header (the SocketJack framing format).

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.WebSocketProtocolHandler
member.summary: Detects WebSocket upgrade requests and manages WebSocket (RFC 6455) connections inside a . After completing the HTTP upgrade handshake, incoming WebSocket frames are decoded, deserialized through the SocketJack serializer, and dispatched via . This allows browser-based WebSocket clients to share the same port as SocketJack and HTTP clients.

member.name: F:SocketJack.Net.WebSocketProtocolHandler._states
member.summary: Per-connection state for the WebSocket protocol.

member.name: M:SocketJack.Net.WebSocketProtocolHandler.CanHandle(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Returns when looks like an HTTP GET request that contains the Upgrade: websocket header.

member.summary: Serializes and sends it as a WebSocket frame directly on 's stream.

member.summary: Sends JavaScript class constructors for all whitelisted types so that browser clients can create typed objects for the SocketJack protocol.

member.name: T:SocketJack.Net.HttpProtocolHandler
member.summary: Detects and processes HTTP requests. Routes are stored in the parent infrastructure so that a single set of mappings is shared regardless of whether requests arrive through the protocol router or the base class directly.

summary.para: Use compression for network transfer.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UseTerminatedStreams
member.summary:
Send and receive objects with terminator (default is ).
to parse objects. An exception would be used in the case of HttpServer where the terminator is not used. HttpServer simply reads to the end of the stream.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UseSsl
member.summary: Use SSL for network transfer.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Chunking
member.summary:
When enabled, outbound messages are buffered and flushed in large chunks at the interval specified by .
This reduces the number of small writes and syscalls at the cost of added latency.
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ChunkingIntervalMs
member.summary:
The interval in milliseconds between chunked flushes when is enabled.
Default is 100 ms.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ChunkingAutomaticLatencyScaling
member.summary:
When , the library automatically adjusts based on measured round-trip latency so chunk flushes stay proportional to network delay.
Requires the built-in ping loop to be active (client-side only).
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Fps
member.summary: Gets or sets the frame rate, in frames per second, for processing operations.
member.remarks: Setting this property adjusts the timing interval used for frame updates. The value should be a positive integer to ensure correct timing behavior. 0 or less = disabled.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Whitelist
member.summary: Types that are allowed to be deserialized.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.Blacklist
member.summary: Types that are not allowed to be deserialized.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.MaxDatagramSize
member.summary:
Maximum datagram payload size in bytes for UDP.
Datagrams exceeding this size will be rejected with an error.
Default is 65,507 bytes (maximum safe UDP payload). Lower to ~1,400 for safe MTU.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ClientTimeoutSeconds
member.summary:
Timeout in seconds before a UDP server considers a client disconnected due to inactivity.
Default is 30 seconds.

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.UdpReceiveBufferSize
member.summary:
Size of the receive buffer used by the UDP socket in bytes.
Default is 65,535 bytes (maximum single UDP datagram size including headers).

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.EnableBroadcast
member.summary:
When enabled, the UDP socket is configured to allow sending broadcast datagrams.
Default is .

member.name: P:SocketJack.Net.NetworkOptions.ClientTimeoutCheckIntervalMs
member.summary:
Interval in milliseconds between client timeout checks on the UDP server.
Default is 5000 ms (5 seconds).
